results.title: AcmeVendor - Security Intake Review
results.snippet: AcmeVendor security intake page detailing multi-factor authentication, data encryption, annual audits, and breach notification procedures.

pages.title: AcmeVendor - Security Intake Review
pages.content:
AcmeVendor Security Intake

This document provides an overview of AcmeVendor's security practices for the intake review process.

1. Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA): All user accounts accessing AcmeVendor production systems are protected with hardware-backed MFA. Administrative accounts require phishing-resistant FIDO2 keys. MFA is enforced for VPN, email, and administrative consoles.

2. Encryption: AcmeVendor encrypts data at rest using AES-256. Data in transit is protected with TLS 1.3. Customer data stored in cloud environments is encrypted with customer-managed keys. Database encryption is enabled for all production databases.

3. Audits: AcmeVendor undergoes annual SOC 2 Type II audits and quarterly penetration tests. Audit reports are available under NDA to enterprise customers upon request. Internal security reviews are conducted monthly, and findings are tracked in a centralized risk register.

4. Breach Notification: AcmeVendor maintains a 24-hour breach notification policy. In the event of a confirmed data breach involving customer data, affected customers will be notified within 24 hours of discovery. The incident response team is available 24/7 via a dedicated hotline.

5. Additional Security Measures: Role-based access control, network segmentation, continuous vulnerability scanning, and a bug bounty program are in place.

For further details, contact security-assess@acmevendor.example.com.
